headline: How has the Spirit exit changed competitive intensity in the airline industry?
summary: Investing.com -- The exit of Spirit Airlines from the U.S. market has eased competitive pressure across the airline industry, creating conditions that could boost annual industry revenue by as much as $2.3 billion, according to a recent analysis.

content.title: How has the Spirit exit changed competitive intensity in the airline industry?

headline: Is United Airlines (UAL) Using New St. Croix Route To Quietly Reframe Its Caribbean Strategy?
summary: United Airlines has announced that from October 31, 2026 it will operate a new Saturday-only nonstop service between St. Croix, U.S. Virgin Islands and Newark/New York on a 126-seat Boeing 737-700, becoming the only carrier linking St. Croix directly to the New York City area. This added route expands United’s Caribbean network from Newark/New York to 23 destinations and, alongside its existing St. Thomas service, strengthens the airline’s role in supporting island-hopping itineraries across...
